requirements.part_name: Garment Bundle Transfer Conveyor Module - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Garment Bundle Transfer Conveyor Module'. The
broader use case is: A textile factory station where bundled cut pieces move between sewing
preparation and assembly cells. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: modular
conveyor bed with side guide rails and bundle support paddles All other product elements are
external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role:
Carries soft stacked garment bundles while keeping their orientation controlled during handoff. It
is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Soft goods support, repeating conveyor
elements, guide clearances, and transfer interfaces create a mix of rigid machine geometry and
fabric-handling intent. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using
rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all
datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into
that same solid piece.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 95 mm x 85 mm x 69 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 5 mm on the length centerline at X=17 mm and X=78 mm, Y=42 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 31 mm long x 15 mm wide through the part, centered at X=47 mm, Y=42 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 25 mm x 28 mm x 33 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Fabric Roll Loading Cradle'. The broader use case
is: A cutting room setup where fabric rolls are staged before spreading onto an automated cutting
table. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: angled cradle frame with roller
supports and adjustable end stops All other product elements are external reference items and must
not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Supports and aligns a fabric roll so it
can unwind smoothly into downstream spreading equipment. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD
limit corpus because: Cylindrical roll support, tangential contact surfaces, adjustable stops, and
ergonomic loading access require clear representation of motion and contact. The part is made from
low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round
metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and
radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 82 mm and length 97 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 20 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 30 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 27 mm wide over 89 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M6 holes through the top flat at X=32 mm and X=64 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 6 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Soft Goods Transfer Paddle'. The broader use case
is: A robotic garment handling station moving fabric panels between conveyors and fixtures. The
chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: thin paddle plate with tapered leading edge
and low-friction surface zones All other product elements are external reference items and must not
be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Slides beneath soft textile pieces and
transfers them with minimal wrinkling. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Very thin functional geometry, tapered insertion profile, surface zones, and textile
interaction clearances test subtle but important CAD details. The part is made from 6061-T6 aluminum
using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile,
machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called
out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 190 mm x 130 mm x 12 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 12 mm on the length centerline at X=26 mm and X=164 mm, Y=65 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 63 mm long x 14 mm wide through the part, centered at X=95 mm, Y=65 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 47 mm x 43 mm x 1 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: machined angled reference face
functional_features.description: Machine one top reference face at 30 degrees over the last 38 mm of length.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.

Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Apparel Work Ticket Clip Rail'. The broader use
case is: A sewing line where each garment bundle travels with a process ticket and material samples.
The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: conveyor-side clip rail with flexible
ticket retainers All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled.
The part must perform this mechanical role: Holds work tickets beside moving garment bundles without
blocking fabric access. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Small
repeating clip features, document clearance, conveyor attachment, and operator readability angle
require detailed accessory modeling. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent
using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile,
machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called
out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 135 mm x 120 mm x 10 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 5 mm on the length centerline at X=24 mm and X=111 mm, Y=60 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 45 mm long x 10 mm wide through the part, centered at X=67 mm, Y=60 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 33 mm x 40 mm x 5 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: machined angled reference face
functional_features.description: Machine one top reference face at 21 degrees over the last 27 mm of length.

functional_features.name: integral hook lip
functional_features.description: Leave an integral hook lip on one short end, projecting 16 mm and undercut 3 mm for registration.
